focus: Senior tension, terrified of driver, slicer equipment seeking, embarrassing driver struggles

PAIN.quote: I'm 63 and a higher handicap (17) looking to get off the tee and in the fairway more often. I find I tense up and get erractic with my 910 D2
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

PAIN.quote: I beleive my problem is tension and I'm wondering if a real light driver like the Cleveland 270 would help me relax and swing smoother
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

PAIN.quote: I remain off the fairway even with help from a pro
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

PAIN.quote: He gets very nervous on the tee box especially when people are watching, and it gets in his head
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

PAIN.quote: One thing that will increase tension is standing over the ball too long, it leaves too much time for negative thoughts to creep in
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

PAIN.quote: I've been playing golf for about ten years now and have yet to play a driver that has helped me turn my small slice into a playable fade
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

PAIN.quote: I'm currently using a ping G425 SFT. It's ok but my drives aren't consistent
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
PAIN.context: Draw bias driver not providing consistency

PAIN.quote: In my first 10 years I had a very nice slice, thank you very much!! It was a PITA to play consistently; left to right... but every once in awhile it would be a straight left shot which always meant trouble
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
PAIN.context: Decade-long slice struggle with two-way miss

PAIN.quote: I made a decision to either get rid of the slice or quit playing altogether
PAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

PAIN.quote: I feel your pain. I cycle through hitting my driver great and hitting it so bad that I leave it at home
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver
PAIN.context: Cycling driver confidence - leave at home

PAIN.quote: I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver. Back in November/December I was driving the ball damn well, to the point where I stepped up and expected to hit the ball 250ish in the fairway. Fast forward to this past weekend, and good f'ing god it's a mess
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver
PAIN.context: Sudden complete loss of driver ability

HOPE.quote: It really only took a couple of lessons with the pro (but many hours of practice) to fix my swing. My tendency now is a straight ball with sometimes a little fade; that's very easy to play
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

HOPE.quote: To cure his wayward drives, Dad's developed a unique routine. He gets on the tee box with his driver and glove in his hand, and at the start of his routine he puts on his glove. Doesn't take a practice swing, and immediately swings away. The act of putting on his glove takes away the nervous energy
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

HOPE.quote: I in reducing, and eventually eliminating my 15-25 yard fade, realized I was still setting up for the fade swing which was necessary to play the fade that I had compensated for for years. I finally began to trust the club and set up neutral or even with a little draw set up, and have the confidence to aim down the middle
HOPE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
HOPE.context: Trust the club + neutral setup = confidence

HOPE.quote: Sounds like you really just need to relax and find a good routine that involves picking a target, set up, breathing, and tempo
HOPE.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: You need to learn how to release the club head so that it will be square at impact. A slice is the result of having a club face that is open in relationship to your swing path
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: You have a swing flaw that is causing a bad path to face ratio. More than likely coming over the top with and open face
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Over the top + open face = slice

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Because depending on how bad the face to path relationship is or how bad the path is there isn't a driver that will fix the issue
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Path/face too severe for equipment fix

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: A draw bias driver just means the sweet spot is more towards the heel. That won't help if there are toe shots with and open face
ROOT_CAUSE.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I switch from the blue stiff shaft to the softer tip red shaft in regular (ss=93). This has taken away some over swinging like with the stiff and has increased trajectory; however I remain off the fairway
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Lessons, lessons, and more lessons. I gave in myself this year and have opted for the pros advice. You already have the driver that should reduce slices. Consistency is about having a repeatable swing
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I tried but was unable to figure out why I was doing and had to get lessons
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I would also expect that the player has tried to fix their swing otherwise they wouldn't have been trying to find another alternative to straighten out the ball flight
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Equipment search after swing fix failed

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Playing draw based drivers should help you reduce the amount of right to left curve; Callaway Rogue ST Max D might be a good option. If you really want to reduce your left to right curve, a swing fix might be your best option
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias reduces but doesn't fix

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: I don't think a driver will fix your issue. If you have a fade that stays in play, a draw-based driver will reduce the amount of fade but it won't change a slice into a fade
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias limitation - won't convert slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: But if you are looking for a band aid instead of a fix the Ping sft is what you are looking for. It won't fix the issue but it will help reduce the negative effects
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Draw bias = band aid not fix

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: There are no drivers that are going to fix that. Fixing the swing will fix it
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: No driver fixes slice - swing only

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Although I am not a senior, a club that is too light will be harder to control compared to a club that is too heavy, in my experience
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee

VILLAIN.quote: Searching for a magic club to fix it is the wrong approach
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

VILLAIN.quote: The problem with today's society is that we all look for the QUICK FIX. We want to blame something else instead of ourselves. Look, you are a golfer, golf is hard and takes a long time to get good
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

VILLAIN.quote: It's not the driver that will fix any problem, it's the swing path or an open/closed face at impact. Now, don't get me wrong, a biased driver may help a bit, but it's not the end all be all solution
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing

VILLAIN.quote: Why does a question about a product have to end up in a response for lessons? If that was needed, he could have posted in the swing section
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
VILLAIN.context: Forum frustration - always pushed to lessons

VILLAIN.quote: I have also viewed the horrors of some on this site looking for that 'perfect' golf swing, transition move, blah, blah, blah
VILLAIN.source: MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer

sources_covered: GolfWRX - Can a lighter weight driver help a senior relax on the tee
MyGolfSpy - Need driver recommendation for a slicer
MyGolfSpy - Draw biased drivers vs working on swing
TheSandTrap - I have officially lost the ability to hit my driver
high_value_themes_identified: Draw bias = band aid not cure
No driver will fix severe slice
Standing over ball increases negative thoughts
Forum frustration - always told to get lessons
